summary: Execute Rwanda Revenue Authority follow-up now to secure a 30-minute discovery/demo this week; use prior plan; adjust for after-hours; report status within 2 hours.
reasoning: Highest-impact overdue sales action with idle agents; previous decision remains unexecuted/logged. Acting now advances revenue and clears backlog while reusing the existing playbook to avoid repetition.

task.title: RRA follow-up: call (next Kigali business hours) and email now to book 30-minute discovery/demo this week
task.description: Use the previously defined RRA follow-up plan with these constraints: 1) Review CRM/account notes and last correspondence; verify primary and alternate contacts. 2) If within Kigali business hours (08:30–17:30 CAT), call the primary contact now; if unavailable, leave voicemail and try an alternate via the RRA switchboard within 10 minutes. If outside business hours, skip calling for now. 3) Send a concise follow-up email immediately proposing three time slots within the next 3 business days; offer a one-pager and a relevant case study. 4) On contact, confirm needs, stakeholders, decision process, timeline, and any procurement/compliance requirements; capture objections. 5) Log all attempts and outcomes in the CRM; either book the meeting or schedule the next follow-up within 48 hours with blockers noted. 6) If numbers are invalid or email bounces, immediately flag specific gaps for research enrichment. 7) If a call was skipped due to after-hours, schedule it for 08:30 CAT next business day. 8) Post a brief status update upon completion—deadline: within 2 hours.
